interactions.summary: Jiang says Bismarck made Germany powerful by caring for soldiers and workers as war assets, then introduces Mussolini's myth of the nation as the peak nation-state form and has Ivory read the myth passage.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang frames and interprets; Ivory reads Mussolini in seg-0027.

interactions.summary: He argues that World War II was won by destroying productive capacity and civilian populations, then ties population growth, medicine, sanitation, consumer capitalism, Ukraine, and coming World War III into one nation-state war logic.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_00 is Ivory reading a Mussolini passage about myth and nation.

claims.claim: Jiang says Bismarck understood that a nation-state must make warriors and keep workers healthy because workers produce the machines of war.
He argues Mussolini understood the nation-state's real purpose as fighting wars and that a mythology of war can make people happily fight and die.
Jiang claims World War II was won by the Allies because they could destroy German and Japanese productive capacity while America and the Soviet Union preserved theirs.
He argues that the nation-state idea became so powerful that defeating it required destroying civilian populations, not merely killing soldiers.
Jiang says medicine and sanitation developed because the nation-state willed science and technology toward growing populations needed for war.
He identifies postwar American consumer capitalism as a unique system that grew the global population from about two billion to about eight billion and generated enormous wealth, including in China.
He predicts that because the consumer-capitalist system is unstable and there are too many people, at least half of humanity will die in World War III.
He classifies the war in Ukraine as essentially a 19th-century war or World War I-style war focused on killing soldiers.

signature_moments.moment: The nation becomes a myth that makes war meaningful enough to die for.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It names the ideological peak of the nation-state before Jiang turns to total war.

signature_moments.moment: The soldier is no longer the real target; the civilian population becomes the war-winning target.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we need to destroy the civilian population
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is Jiang's hinge from citizen armies to total war.

signature_moments.moment: Science and medicine are pulled into the state will to make more war-capable people.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the nation state willed these things to happen
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes modern progress subordinate to the nation-state's population strategy.

signature_moments.moment: The coming war is framed as a population die-off, not merely a geopolitical contest.
signature_moments.source_phrase: at least 50 % of humanity will die off in World War III
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is one of the lecture's starkest predictions and should survive public compression.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A created faith or passion that subordinates everything else to national greatness and makes war meaningful.
A 20th-century war form in which victory comes from destroying factories, infrastructure, and civilian productive capacity.
The American-led postwar game that Jiang says expanded wealth and population but became unstable and unsustainable.

chronology_notes.note: As of 2026-04-28, Jiang classifies Ukraine as an older-style soldier-killing war and reserves 'first 21st-century war' for Iran.

uncertainty_notes.note: The claim that millions are dying in Ukraine is Jiang's statement in the source and should not be treated as externally verified by this packet pass.
